hardQuestionAnswer: Batch 25 found that protocol-vs-random split deltas should not be treated as leakage by default. The executed targets did not produce direct duplicate, group, or target-leakage evidence sufficient to explain the deltas; class imbalance, ordinary protocol difficulty, and ambiguity remain stronger explanations.
nextBatch26Hypotheses: class imbalance plus metric choice mimics leakage
title: Batch 25 Leakage-Risk Cards Week 1
whatSovrynLearned: Sovryn learned that Leakage-Risk Cards can separate testable leakage checks from not-testable group/file fields, preventing split-risk evidence from turning into fake leakage detection.
